what_it_does: This JSON file is a structured interpretation/audit record for the slice-1 token audit of the claude-ds project. It documents a mid-audit correction about the logo gold, maps consumers that hardcode gold/bronze hex values, records the additive-only action taken, and lists the next deliberate steps.
observations: The file records that --accent-gold #E0C010 is the sampled logo gold from the actual ConceptV logo PNG and must not be softened or overwritten.
It distinguishes the invariant brand gold/bronze from softer applied ramp stops used in decks, proposing to add --ramp-1..4 instead of changing existing tokens.
It lists specific files that hardcode gold/bronze hex values as a de-hardcode backlog.
It notes that Layouts.jsx already contains blueprint-ghost, gold-grid, and diamond watermark artifacts, which contradicts the README's 'flat' description.
It marks assets/icons/CvIcon.jsx as token-correct and preview/* plus app/services/openai.js brand prompts as hardcoded but lower risk.
The slice-1 action is additive only: add --ramp-1..4 to colors_and_type.css while leaving logo gold and bronze untouched.
It defers zone-ladder pigment retuning to a measured pass that compares computed outputs against sampled targets such as #fdfcf7 and #f8f8f8.
Next steps include a de-hardcode pass, a measured zone-ladder retune, and reconciling status chips with existing warm status dots.
standouts: The audit reversed a planned softening of the gold after identifying it as a sampled brand asset, recording the correction before any color value was changed.
It surfaces an existing contradiction: Layouts.jsx already contains texture/ghost/grid elements despite the README calling the system flat.
It enforces strict additive-only discipline and refuses to retune the zone ladder until computed output can be measured against samples.
conventions: Additive-only change discipline: add ramp tokens without overwriting brand assets.
Single-home / de-hardcode backlog: catalog hardcoded consumers so recalibration can propagate from one token home.
Defer-until-measured: avoid guessing pigment retunes; wait for computed-vs-sampled comparison.
Evidence-before-fix: cite exact files and sampled provenance, including the 234k-px logo source.
Reconcile-don't-fork: extend existing status dots rather than creating a parallel system.
concerns: The ~10-consumer de-hardcoding backlog is explicitly deferred, leaving a load-bearing drift risk until a later pass.
Line-range references such as --status-* lines 59-68 can drift as colors_and_type.css evolves.
notes: Slice-1 token audit record that protects the sampled logo gold/bronze as invariant brand assets, adds an applied ramp instead, and maps the remaining hardcoded-hex consumers for a future de-hardcode pass.
questions: Has the de-hardcode pass for the ~10-consumer backlog been executed, or does it still stand?
What additional sampled targets beyond #fdfcf7/#f8f8f8 will define the measured zone-ladder retune?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a scoped slice-1 token-and-consumer audit that delivers the correction, consumer map, action taken, and next steps for exactly that scope.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to recalibrate the token layer toward the source design DNA without damaging genuine brand assets or churning consumers blindly, by separating invariant sampled colors from applied ramp stops, cataloguing every hardcoded consumer, and deferring uncertain retunes until they can be measured.
novelty: Notable for being an audit that reverses its own parent plan on fresh evidence and for discovering that the supposedly flat system already contains texture artifacts worth mining, turning recalibration into a surgical, provenance-anchored operation.
contribution: Contributes the token-layer audit findings: the invariant sampled gold/bronze vs the additive ramp stops, a file-level hardcoded-hex consumer backlog, the additive --ramp-1..4 action taken, and the deferred measured zone-retune plan — the provenance and drift map a fused system would keep to know which color values are sacred and where drift remains.
summary_for_embedding: JSON audit record for slice-1 of the claude-ds design system, stating that logo gold #E0C010 and bronze #988058 are sampled brand assets to preserve, adding applied ramp stops --ramp-1..4, mapping files that hardcode gold/bronze hex, and deferring zone-ladder retune to a measured pass.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to recalibrate design tokens toward source DNA without overwriting brand assets or churning consumers, by separating invariant sampled colors from applied ramp stops, cataloguing hardcoded-hex drift, and postponing retuning until output can be measured against samples.
